pdg_node.informal_desc: Initializes the state for the GLFT model coefficients.

declaration.name: initialize_glft_state
declaration.docstring: Initializes the state for the GLFT model coefficients.
declaration.conceptual_summary: initialize_2d_scalar_state. Generates a fixed, two-dimensional state vector represented by two independent scalar floating-point values. This function acts as a factory, providing a deterministic, hardcoded starting point for any system or model requiring a two-parameter initial condition.. Applications: Initializing the starting (x, y) coordinates for a particle in a 2D particle filter for robot localization., Setting the initial proportional and integral gain parameters for a PI controller in a feedback control system before an auto-tuning process begins., Defining the initial population sizes for two interacting species in a Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model., Establishing the starting weights for a two-neuron, single-layer perceptron in a simple neural network.

declaration.name: initialize_glft_state
declaration.docstring: Initializes the state for the GLFT model coefficients.
declaration.conceptual_summary: initialize_2d_scalar_state. Generates a fixed, two-dimensional state vector represented by two independent scalar floating-point values. This function acts as a factory, providing a deterministic, hardcoded starting point for any system or model requiring a two-parameter initial condition.. pdg_node.statement: (last_c1: float, last_c2: float, xi: float, gamma: float, delta: float, A: float, k: float) -> tuple[float, float]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Updates the GLFT model coefficients based on market parameters and the previous state. This embodies the core state transition.

declaration.name: update_glft_coefficients
declaration.type_signature: (last_c1: float, last_c2: float, xi: float, gamma: float, delta: float, A: float, k: float) -> tuple[float, float]
declaration.docstring: Updates the GLFT model coefficients based on market parameters and the previous state. This embodies the core state transition.
declaration.conceptual_summary: iterative_coupled_scalar_update. Calculates the subsequent state of a 2-dimensional system from its preceding state. The transformation is a deterministic function of the two previous state components and a set of scalar control parameters, modeling a single step in a discrete-time evolution.. Applications: Simulating predator-prey population dynamics, where the two state variables represent the populations of two interacting species., Modeling the concentration of two chemical species in a reaction network over a discrete time step., Updating the position and velocity of a simulated physical object in a 2D physics engine, subject to forces defined by the control parameters., Evolving the activation level and fatigue state of a neuron in a computational neuroscience model.

pdg_node.statement: (half_spread: float, c1: float, threshold: float) -> bool
pdg_node.informal_desc: Checks if the ratio of the half-spread to the c1 coefficient is below a specified threshold, optimizing the balance between execution probability and adverse selection risk.
